zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan Beaumont於1283年出生在英國英格蘭素福爾可伍德布里奇時，她的父親Richard de Beaumont24歲，母親Alice de Beaumont20歲。她於1303年與Sir Giles de Braose Lord Stinton在英國英格蘭諾福爾可結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。她去世於1332年在英國英格蘭奧可福爾艾尤爾梅享年49歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan Beaumont于1283年出生在英国英格兰素福爾可伍德布里奇时，她的父亲Richard de Beaumont24岁，母亲Alice de Beaumont20岁。她于1303年与Sir Giles de Braose Lord Stinton在英国英格兰諾福爾可结婚。他们至少是3个儿子的父母。她去世于1332年在英国英格兰奧可福爾艾尤爾梅享年49岁。

nameMeanings.name: Beaumont
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Gilles, Gaetan, Honore, Jacques, Jean-Guy, Marcel.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of the five places in Normandy or several others elsewhere in France so named. The placename comes from Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + mont ‘hill’. There are also places in England so named under Norman influence in Cumberland, Lancashire, and Essex, the last of which changed its name in the 12th century from Fulepet ‘foul pit’ to Bealmont ‘beautiful hill’; these may also have given rise to the surname. The surname is now widespread throughout England, but most common in Yorkshire. Compare Belmont .

History: Many English bearers of this surname are descended from Henry de Beaumont (died 1340), who came to England from France as a soldier, serving Kings Edward I, II, and III of England. He acquired the earldom of Buchan in Scotland by marriage. — Many American bearers of this surname are descendants of John Beaumont (1612–47), who came to North America from England in 1630. William Beaumont came from England to Cambridge MA in 1635 and subsequently moved to CT.

variants.name: Bement
meanings: English (Cambridgeshire): variant of   Beaumont  . This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Beeman
meanings: English (Kent): occupational name for a beekeeper, from Middle English bee-man ‘bee-keeper’ (Old English bēo + mann).
Americanized form of German Biemann or of its Dutch cognate Bieman.

variants.name: Belmont
meanings: French and English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beaumont  .
Catalan: habitational name from the placename Bellmont ‘beautiful mountain’. Compare Spanish   Belmonte  .

variants.name: Baumunk
meanings: German: probably a Germanized form of a French Huguenot name Beaumont (see also Beaumont ).

variants.name: Bayman
meanings: Americanized form of German Beumann or Bäumann, variants of Baumann .

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a by‐form of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

en.lifeSummary: When Joan Beaumont was born in 1283, in Woodbridge, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom, her father, Richard de Beaumont, was 24 and her mother, Alice de Beaumont, was 20. She married Sir Giles de Braose Lord Stinton in 1303, in Norfolk, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. She died in 1332, in Ewelme, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 49.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan Beaumont est née en 1283, à Woodbridge, Suffolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Richard de Beaumont, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Alice de Beaumont, avait 20 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Giles de Braose Lord Stinton en 1303, à Norfolk, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Elle est décédée en 1332, à Ewelme, Oxfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 49 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan Beaumont 1283 in Woodbridge, Suffolk, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Richard de Beaumont 24 und ihre Mutter Alice de Beaumont 20. Sie hat 1303 in Norfolk, England, United Kingdom Sir Giles de Braose Lord Stinton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne. Sie starb 1332 im Alter von 49 Jahren in Ewelme, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Beaumont è nata il nel 1283 nel Woodbridge, Suffolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, Richard de Beaumont, aveva 24 anni e sua madre, Alice de Beaumont, aveva 20 anni. Lei sposato Sir Giles de Braose Lord Stinton nel 1303, nel Norfolk, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli. Morì nel 1332, nel Ewelme, Oxfordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 49 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan Beaumontが1283年にイギリスイギリススフォルクウッドブリッジで生まれた時，父Richard de Beaumontは24歳，母Alice de Beaumontは20歳であった。1303年にイギリスイギリスノーフォルクでSir Giles de Braose Lord Stintonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子の親であった。彼女は1332年にイギリスイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアエウエルメで49歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Beaumont(이)가 1283년 영국 잉글랜드 수폴크 우드브리지에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Richard de Beaumont(은)는 24세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Alice de Beaumont(은)는 20세였다. 그녀는 1303년 영국 잉글랜드 노르폴크에서 Sir Giles de Braose Lord Stinton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1332년 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르 웰에서 49세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Beaumont nasceu em 1283, em Woodbridge, Suffolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Richard de Beaumont, tinha 24 anos e sua mãe, Alice de Beaumont, tinha 20 anos. Ela casou-se com Sir Giles de Braose Lord Stinton em 1303, em Norfolk, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos. Ela faleceu em 1332, em Ewelme, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 49 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan Beaumont родилась в 1283 г. в местности вудбридж, суффолк, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Richard de Beaumont было 24 года, а матери, которую звали Alice de Beaumont, 20. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Giles de Braose Lord Stinton в 1303 г. в местности норфолк, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына. Она умерла в 1332 г. в местности ювелм, оксфордшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 49 лет.

Se casó con Sir Giles de Braose Lord Stinton en 1303, en Norfolk, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos. Murió en 1332, en Ewelme, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 49 años.
